# SBDC Client Intake and Discovery Process for BOSS

> BOSS Libraries public routing record. Use it to find the right source, link, organization, or person.

## Description

Use this document when a BOSS user is likely to benefit from SBDC advising but the model needs enough information to route correctly.

## When to surface

Surface when the user's question concerns business planning, disaster readiness, formation during Ideation, Validation, Formation and this source is relevant to the next document or human handoff.

## Key information

- ## Purpose
- Use this document when a BOSS user is likely to benefit from SBDC advising but the model needs enough information to route correctly.
- ## Minimum intake questions
- Ask one at a time when possible:
- What ZIP/county will the business operate from?
- Are you still exploring the idea, already selling, or already operating with revenue?
- What is the business idea or current business?

## People and links

- Organizations: Small Business Development Center (SBDC)
- Link: <https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/default.asp>

## Next handoff

Open the cited source link when available and route follow-up to Small Business Development Center (SBDC) or the named qualified resource.

## Guardrails

- The BOSS Libraries Librarian retrieves and routes information; it is not a mentor or a substitute for a qualified professional.
- Do not make final legal, tax, accounting, lending, certification, procurement, or compliance decisions for the user.
- Verify live pages for current eligibility, deadlines, contacts, prices, and program rules before presenting them as current.

## Original document

Use **View original** in the BOSS Library to open or download the approved public source.
